---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-22"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

#Tema de los gráficos:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

tema_paper <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14, family = "serif"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )
```




##Gráficos

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

tema_paper <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14, family = "serif"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

ruta_298 <- "/home/alumno16/MM/ejercicio_tripeptido/4-analysis/298K"
ruta_400 <- "/home/alumno16/MM/ejercicio_tripeptido/4-analysis/400K"

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 1. ENERGIA CINETICA
# -------------------------------------------------------
data_ec_298 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_298, "kinetic.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_ec_298) <- c("Tiempo", "Energia")

data_ec_400 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_400, "kinetic.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_ec_400) <- c("Tiempo", "Energia")

p_ec_298 <- ggplot(data_ec_298, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Energia)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Energia Cinetica vs Tiempo (298 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Energia (kJ/mol)") +
  tema_paper

p_ec_400 <- ggplot(data_ec_400, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Energia)) +
  geom_line(color = "orange") +
  labs(title = "Energia Cinetica vs Tiempo (400 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Energia (kJ/mol)") +
  tema_paper

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "energia_cinetica_298.svg"), p_ec_298, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(ruta_400, "energia_cinetica_400.svg"), p_ec_400, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 2. ENERGIA TOTAL
# -------------------------------------------------------
data_et_298 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_298, "energy.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_et_298) <- c("Tiempo", "Energia")

data_et_400 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_400, "energy.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_et_400) <- c("Tiempo", "Energia")

p_et_298 <- ggplot(data_et_298, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Energia)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Energia Total vs Tiempo (298 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Energia (kJ/mol)") +
  tema_paper

p_et_400 <- ggplot(data_et_400, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Energia)) +
  geom_line(color = "purple") +
  labs(title = "Energia Total vs Tiempo (400 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Energia (kJ/mol)") +
  tema_paper

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "energia_total_298.svg"), p_et_298, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(ruta_400, "energia_total_400.svg"), p_et_400, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 3. TEMPERATURA vs TIEMPO
# -------------------------------------------------------
data_temp_298 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_298, "temp.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_temp_298) <- c("Tiempo", "Temperatura")

data_temp_400 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_400, "temp.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_temp_400) <- c("Tiempo", "Temperatura")

p_temp_298 <- ggplot(data_temp_298, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Temperatura)) +
  geom_line(color = "pink") +
  labs(title = "Temperatura vs Tiempo (298 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Temperatura (K)") +
  tema_paper

p_temp_400 <- ggplot(data_temp_400, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Temperatura)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Temperatura vs Tiempo (400 K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Temperatura (K)") +
  tema_paper

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "temperatura_298.svg"), p_temp_298, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(ruta_400, "temperatura_400.svg"), p_temp_400, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 4. HISTOGRAMA TEMPERATURA
# -------------------------------------------------------
p_hist_298 <- ggplot(data_temp_298, aes(x = Temperatura)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "blue", bins = 30, alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Histograma de Temperatura (298 K)",
       x = "Temperatura (K)", y = "Frecuencia") +
  tema_paper

p_hist_400 <- ggplot(data_temp_400, aes(x = Temperatura)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "red", bins = 30, alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Histograma de Temperatura (400 K)",
       x = "Temperatura (K)", y = "Frecuencia") +
  tema_paper

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "histograma_temp_298.svg"), p_hist_298, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(ruta_400, "histograma_temp_400.svg"), p_hist_400, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 5. DISTANCIAS DE ENLACE
# -------------------------------------------------------
leer_xvg <- function(archivo) {
  lineas <- readLines(archivo)
  datos <- read.table(text = paste(lineas[!grepl("^[@#]", lineas)],
             collapse = "\n"), header = FALSE, fill = TRUE)
  colnames(datos) <- c("Tiempo", "Enlace1", "Enlace2")
  return(datos)
}

datos_dist_298 <- leer_xvg(file.path(ruta_298, "dist.xvg"))
datos_dist_400 <- leer_xvg(file.path(ruta_400, "dist.xvg"))

p_dist <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = datos_dist_298, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Enlace1, color = "298K - C=O")) +
  geom_line(data = datos_dist_298, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Enlace2, color = "298K - C-N")) +
  geom_line(data = datos_dist_400, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Enlace1, color = "400K - C=O"), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(data = datos_dist_400, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Enlace2, color = "400K - C-N"), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Comparacion de Distancias de Enlace (298K vs 400K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Distancia (nm)", color = "Condicion") +
  tema_paper

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "distancias_enlace.svg"), p_dist, width = 10, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 6. ANGULOS DE ENLACE
# -------------------------------------------------------
leer_angulo <- function(archivo) {
  lineas <- readLines(archivo)
  datos <- read.table(text = paste(lineas[!grepl("^[@#]", lineas)],
             collapse = "\n"), header = FALSE)
  colnames(datos) <- c("Tiempo", "Angulo")
  return(datos)
}

datos_ang1_298 <- leer_angulo(file.path(ruta_298, "ang_ca_c_o.xvg"))
datos_ang2_298 <- leer_angulo(file.path(ruta_298, "ang_n_ca_c.xvg"))
datos_ang1_400 <- leer_angulo(file.path(ruta_400, "ang_ca_c_o.xvg"))
datos_ang2_400 <- leer_angulo(file.path(ruta_400, "ang_n_ca_c.xvg"))

datos_ang1_298$Grupo <- "298K - CA-C-O"
datos_ang2_298$Grupo <- "298K - N-CA-C"
datos_ang1_400$Grupo <- "400K - CA-C-O"
datos_ang2_400$Grupo <- "400K - N-CA-C"

datos_ang <- rbind(datos_ang1_298, datos_ang2_298, datos_ang1_400, datos_ang2_400)

p_ang <- ggplot(datos_ang, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Angulo, color = Grupo)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7, size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Comparacion de Angulos de Enlace (298K vs 400K)",
       subtitle = "Evolucion temporal de los angulos seleccionados",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Angulo (grados)", color = "Condiciones") +
  tema_paper +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "angulos_enlace.svg"), p_ang, width = 10, height = 6)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 7. ANGULOS DIEDROS
# -------------------------------------------------------
leer_dihedros <- function(archivo) {
  datos <- read.table(archivo, header = FALSE)
  colnames(datos) <- c("Tiempo", "Phi", "Psi")
  return(datos)
}

ala2_298 <- leer_dihedros(file.path(ruta_298, "ala2.dat"))
ala2_400 <- leer_dihedros(file.path(ruta_400, "ala2.dat"))

ala2_298$Grupo <- "ALA-2 (298K)"
ala2_400$Grupo <- "ALA-2 (400K)"
datos_dih <- rbind(ala2_298, ala2_400)

p_phi <- ggplot(datos_dih, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Phi, color = Grupo)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Evolucion del Angulo Dihedro Phi (298K vs 400K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Phi (grados)") +
  tema_paper +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_psi <- ggplot(datos_dih, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Psi, color = Grupo)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Evolucion del Angulo Dihedro Psi (298K vs 400K)",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Psi (grados)") +
  tema_paper +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "diedro_phi.svg"), p_phi, width = 8, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "diedro_psi.svg"), p_psi, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 8. RADIO DE GIRO
# -------------------------------------------------------
data_gyr_298 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_298, "gyrate.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_gyr_298) <- c("Tiempo", "Radio_Giro", "Rx", "Ry", "Rz")

data_gyr_400 <- read.table(file.path(ruta_400, "gyrate.xvg"), comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data_gyr_400) <- c("Tiempo", "Radio_Giro", "Rx", "Ry", "Rz")

data_gyr_298$Grupo <- "298K"
data_gyr_400$Grupo <- "400K"
datos_gyr <- rbind(data_gyr_298, data_gyr_400)

p_gyr <- ggplot(datos_gyr, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Radio_Giro, color = Grupo)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.8, size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Evolucion del Radio de Giro",
       subtitle = "Comparacion entre 298K y 400K",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Radio de Giro (nm)", color = "Temperatura") +
  tema_paper +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "radio_giro.svg"), p_gyr, width = 8, height = 5)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 9. VELOCIDADES DE 5 ATOMOS
# -------------------------------------------------------
leer_xvg_velocidades <- function(archivo) {
  datos <- read.table(archivo, header = FALSE)
  datos <- datos[, c(1, 5, 9, 13, 17, 21)]
  colnames(datos) <- c("Tiempo", "Atomo_1", "Atomo_2", "Atomo_3", "Atomo_4", "Atomo_5")
  datos %>% pivot_longer(cols = -Tiempo, names_to = "Atomo", values_to = "Velocidad")
}

datos_vel_298 <- leer_xvg_velocidades(file.path(ruta_298, "veloc.xvg"))
datos_vel_400 <- leer_xvg_velocidades(file.path(ruta_400, "veloc.xvg"))
datos_vel_298$Temperatura <- "298K"
datos_vel_400$Temperatura <- "400K"
datos_vel <- rbind(datos_vel_298, datos_vel_400)

p_vel <- ggplot(datos_vel, aes(x = Tiempo, y = Velocidad, color = Atomo)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, size = 1) +
  facet_grid(Temperatura ~ Atomo) +
  labs(title = "Evolucion de la Velocidad de 5 Atomos",
       subtitle = "Comparacion entre 298K y 400K",
       x = "Tiempo (ps)", y = "Velocidad (nm/ps)") +
  tema_paper +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave(file.path(ruta_298, "velocidades.svg"), p_vel, width = 14, height = 7)
```


##Gráficos parte opcional

```{r}
setwd("/home/alumno16/MM/ejercicio_tripeptido/5-extended")

tema_paper <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14, family = "serif"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

setwd("/home/alumno16/MM/ejercicio_tripeptido/5-extended")

file_path <- "temp-opcional.xvg"
data <- read.table(file_path, comment.char = "#", header = FALSE)
colnames(data) <- c("Tiempo", "Temperatura")

p_hist <- ggplot(data, aes(x = Temperatura)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "green", bins = 30, alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Histograma de Temperatura (298 K)",
    x = "Temperatura (K)",
    y = "Frecuencia"
  ) +
  tema_paper

print(p_hist)

ggsave("Hist_temp.svg", plot = p_hist, width = 8, height = 5)


```



```{r}
tema_paper <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14, family = "serif"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

leer_xvg_velocidad <- function(archivo) {
  datos <- read.table(archivo, header = FALSE)[, c(1, 2, 3, 4)]
  colnames(datos) <- c("Tiempo", "Vx", "Vy", "Vz")
  datos %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = c("Vx", "Vy", "Vz"),
      names_to = "Componente",
      values_to = "Velocidad"
    )
}

archivo_velocidad <- "veloc-opcional.xvg"
datos_velocidad <- leer_xvg_velocidad(archivo_velocidad)

graficar_componente <- function(datos, componente, color) {
  ggplot(datos %>% filter(Componente == componente),
         aes(x = Tiempo, y = Velocidad)) +
    geom_line(color = color, alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.1, se = FALSE,
                color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Evolucion de", componente, "del Primer Atomo"),
      x = "Tiempo (ps)",
      y = "Velocidad (nm/ps)"
    ) +
    tema_paper
}

grafico_Vx <- graficar_componente(datos_velocidad, "Vx", "red")
grafico_Vy <- graficar_componente(datos_velocidad, "Vy", "green")
grafico_Vz <- graficar_componente(datos_velocidad, "Vz", "blue")

grafico_final <- grafico_Vx / grafico_Vy / grafico_Vz

print(grafico_final)

ggsave("velocidad_componentes_primer_atomo.svg",
       plot = grafico_final,
       width = 8,
       height = 10)

```

```{r}
datos <- read_table2("rama-opcional.xvg", col_names = c("phi", "psi", "residuo"))

p_rama <- ggplot(datos, aes(x = phi, y = psi)) +
  stat_density_2d_filled(
    aes(fill = after_stat(level)),
    geom = "polygon",
    contour = TRUE,
    bins = 30,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "B", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = expression(phi ~ "(deg)"),
    y = expression(psi ~ "(deg)"),
    title = "Diagramas de Ramachandran por Residuo"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-180, 180), breaks = seq(-180, 180, by = 90)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-180, 180), breaks = seq(-180, 180, by = 90)) +
  facet_wrap(~residuo, ncol = 3) +
  tema_paper +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "cm"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )

ggsave("rama_residuos.svg", plot = p_rama, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 600)
```
